summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, including Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets and the launch of new AI-powered features in various apps. The use of AI in healthcare and education is also on the rise, with AI-powered blood tests and AI-assisted learning tools being developed.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a rise in concerns about AI cheating and the need for more effective methods to detect and prevent it, as seen in the case of the Ivy League professor who ordered an in-person final exam due to suspicions of AI cheating.
highlights: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets, highlighting the growing competition in the AI industry.
New AI-powered features are being launched in various apps, including Waze and Instagram, to improve user experience and provide more personalized services.
The use of AI in healthcare is on the rise, with AI-powered blood tests being developed to help diagnose diseases such as womb cancer.
